title: non-deterministic fail: unwarranted Error-BLKLOOPINIT
body:
---


Author Name: **Ed Spittles**
Original Redmine Issue: 108 from https://www.veripool.org
Original Date: 2009-08-17


---

Hi Wilson

  Trying to run verilator on a module instrumented by the covered tool, I'm seeing some Error-BLKLOOPINIT errors. They are not real: running with --unroll-count 1000 makes no difference.

  The problem is sensitive to the exact text of the verilog file: removing some apparently unrelated statements prior to the failure can make it go away.

  With version 3.713, running a mudflap build with tcmalloc and valgrind, I was able to uncover some bad reads.  Invocation was
```
HEAPCHECK=draconian \
  LD_PRELOAD=/tools/google-perftools/1.3/lib/libprofiler.so \
  MUDFLAP_OPTIONS='-viol-abort -check-initialization -wipe-stack  -wipe-heap -crumple-zone=4096 -free-queue-length=16 -verbose-violations' \
  valgrind /tools/verilator/verilator-3.713/verilator_bin -cc -Wno-WIDTH --unroll-count 1000 loops2-obs.v

```
But I think all the reported problems from that are an artifact of running in such a way.

  I'm sorry the testcase is untidy - it's because it started life as an obfuscated version of covered's annotations, and as I try to reduce it the error keeps going away.  I'll mail it to you directly, for privacy.

  Please let me know if I can be of assistance.

Thanks
Ed
